text: U.S.-China trade tensions, Valleys damdisaster og now COVID-19
have resulted in zero demand growth in dry bulk over the past three years.
Is it time to prepare for more favorable fundamentals in dry bulk going forward?
And how is Golden Ocean, one of the industry leaders positioning themselves for the future?
In this update we'll discuss the outlook for Golden Ocean and the dry bulk market in general.
Velkommen til Utbytte, the DnB podcast, where we explain the financial markets and the global economy.
My name is Marius Brun Haugen from DnB Markets, and today I'm joined by Ulrik Andersen, the CEO of Golden Ocean.
Velkommen Ulrik, how are you?
Thank you very much. I'm good. Happy to be on the show.
I can imagine it has been a busy morning. You came out with numbers for the second quarter today.
How would you summarize Q2?
Ja, you're right. It's been a pretty busy morning. That's how it is in these days.
But I think a good one. Obviously we deliver a net loss of 41 million, which is never satisfying.
But I think given the circumstances, it has been a disastrous quarter for everyone in dry bulk.
Dry bulk and maybe across many industries, I think we are happy with what we have delivered.
You have to keep in mind that we had quite a few capex remaining under our scrub installation program,
which you also had to get through in the second quarter.
That's now behind us, so we are looking forward and we are positive.
That's good. So if you see year to date, you have not been profitable.
But do you hope to be profitable for 2020 as a whole?
Or is it a little too optimistic going to the circumstances?
Or is it the outlook simply too vague to guide on this?
I think we need to remember one thing, and that is that our first half results included a lot of non-cash losses.
Actually almost 122 million.
I'd say that if you adjust for these underlying loss, or for these non-cash items,
then we are looking at an underlying loss of approximately 80 million for the first half.
That's a lot. But how the market is shaping up now and what we are seeing,
I think we are optimistic that we can actually earn that loss back in the second half.
Overall profit, including the non-cash items, I think that might be a little bit too optimistic.
Profitability may be a 2021 story, but what about the dividends?
Are there any closer on the horizon?
I think it's close. It's too soon to say of course, but of course let me be clear.
Returning cash to our shareholders always a priority.
Of course it's a bold decision at the end of the day,
but we work hard and go to the notion every day to get to a position where it's possible.
As I said before, looking at how things are shaping up,
I would say that we are hopefully ready to pay out dividends again sooner rather than later.
OK, Ulrike, you and I, we are not alone today.
We are also joined by our lead shipping analyst Jørgen Lian.
Thanks for joining us Jørgen.
Happy to be here.
Har vi hatt en kans til å se på nummeret igjen, og hva er din inisjon?

Både på Q2-resultene og på Q3-guidene,
som jeg tror er reflektet i stocktraderingen, omkring 4% som vi taler om.

fra kanskje de seneste løpet i 2016.

Ja, det bagerer rettig snart, når kapsliderne valgene

Denne podcasten og detts innehåll skal anses som markedsføringsmaterial fra DMB
og må ikke oppfattes som investeringsanbefaling,
heller som investeringsanalyser.
Innollet skal heller ikke anses for å være investeringsrådgivning, tilpasset den enkelte kundesbehovet.
Informasjonen som gis i podcasten er kun menet som generelt sparverledning og er ikke menet og utgjør juridisk, finansiell, kommersiell, skattemessig eller regnskapsmessig rådgivning.
Innollet i sendingen er ikke gjennomgått eller godkjent av DMB Markets avdeling for analyset.
Alle uttalser reflekterer DMBs vurderinger på tidspunktet for den aktuelle sendingen,
og kan etterkant endres utnærmere beskjed.
DMB er ikke ansvarlig for hvordan informasjonen i podcasten benyttes eller tolkes.
Informasjonen som hennviser til historisk avkassning i finansielle instrumenter, en finansiell indeks eller prognoser,
gir ingen garanti for fremtidig avkassning.
Det bør oppmerksom på at den hver investering et finansiellest instrument er forbundet med risiko for økonomisk tap.
Finansielle instrumenter kan både øke og minnske valg, slik at avkassning kan bli negativ som følge av kursfall.
DMB tar inntil et ansvar for direkte eller indirekte tap og kostnader som måtte oppstå ved bruk av den informasjonen som gis i podcasten.
